title: Puppet Master Requirements Quality Report
description: Schema for the output of the Puppet Master requirements quality analysis pass. Captures verdict, detected issues, auto-applied fixes, and questions requiring user clarification. Verdict is PASS when no blocking issues remain after autofixes; FAIL when any blocking issue is unresolved (not covered by auto_fixes_applied and has a corresponding entry in needs_user_clarification).

verdict.description: Overall quality verdict. PASS = no blocking issues remain after autofixes have been applied. FAIL = at least one blocking issue is unresolved (not in auto_fixes_applied; question surfaced in needs_user_clarification).

requirements_touched.description: List of requirement IDs that were inspected during this quality analysis pass.

generated_at_utc.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which this report was generated.

issues.description: All issues detected across the inspected requirements. Blocking issues fail the verdict unless resolved via auto_fixes_applied.

auto_fixes_applied.description: Fixes that were deterministically applied by the system without requiring user input. Each fix references an issue in issues[].

needs_user_clarification.description: Questions surfaced to the user for issues that cannot be auto-fixed. Each question is specific, answerable, and non-overlapping with other questions in this list.

Issue.title: Issue
Issue.description: A single quality issue detected on a requirement.

issue_id.description: Unique identifier for this issue within the report (e.g. "ISS-001").

category.description: Classification of the issue type detected on the requirement.

requirement_id.description: The ID of the requirement on which this issue was detected.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of what the issue is and why it matters.

severity.description: Impact of this issue on the overall verdict. 'blocking' causes a FAIL verdict if unresolved; 'warning' is informational and does not affect the verdict.

auto_fixable.description: Whether the system can resolve this issue deterministically without user input. If true, a corresponding entry will appear in auto_fixes_applied.

AutoFix.title: AutoFix
AutoFix.description: A fix that was deterministically applied by the system to resolve an issue without user input.

fix_id.description: Unique identifier for this fix within the report (e.g. "FIX-001").

issue_id.description: References the issue_id in issues[] that this fix resolves.

requirement_id.description: The ID of the requirement that was modified or augmented by this fix.

fix_type.description: The kind of automated fix that was applied.

before.description: The original text or field value before the fix was applied. Null or empty string if the fix adds content from nothing (e.g. inserting a new node).

after.description: The replacement or appended text after the fix was applied.

rationale.description: Explanation of why this fix was safe to apply deterministically without user input.

ClarificationQuestion.title: ClarificationQuestion
ClarificationQuestion.description: A question surfaced to the user for an issue that cannot be auto-fixed and requires human judgment.

question_id.description: Unique identifier for this question within the report (e.g. "Q-001").

issue_id.description: References the issue_id in issues[] that this question is intended to resolve.

requirement_id.description: The ID of the requirement this question pertains to.

question.description: A specific, answerable, non-overlapping question for the user. Must be concrete enough that the user's answer can directly resolve the referenced issue.

context.description: Background context to help the user understand why the question is being asked and what information is needed.

answer_format.description: The expected format of the user's answer. Determines how the choices field is interpreted.

choices.description: Enumerated answer options. Required (non-null) when answer_format is 'single_choice' or 'multi_choice'. Must be null for all other answer_format values.
